no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-dc2249e9 — no_changes rationale

What is done
============
The acceptance criterion is already satisfied at the base revision
(d01bc4453e9913335945ea1461485b5c0516a5bb).

  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/executions.spec.ts:362-406

contains the test:

  test('execution detail renders cost tier information', async ({ page }) => { ... })

It loads the execution detail page (/nodes/.../executions/<id>), routes the
NodeInfo / Projects / ExecutionDetail GraphQL operations, asserts the heading
matches the execution id, and asserts the Cost quick-fact cell is present and
renders "$0.0400" — the value fmtCost(0.04) produces from blockExec.costUsd.

The Cost quick-fact cell is rendered unconditionally by the detail page at
cli/.../executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte:188-189 via fmtCost (defined at
:127-130 as `$${c.toFixed(4)}`), so the assertion is grounded in the actual UI.

This test was added in commit 472d4aaf
("test(e2e): verify cost tier info renders in execution detail [ddx-dc2249e9]")
and merged into main via 4bab0a16. It is present on the current base revision.

Why no new commit
=================
The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no test file changes are present") referred to the most recent
attempt (20260501T035858), which produced only an evidence artifact because
the test had already landed in 472d4aaf. The bead was re-queued, but the AC is
already met on main — there is no further test code to add or change in this
worktree without duplicating the existing test.

Adding a duplicate or trivially-renamed test would be churn, not progress.
The existing test fully covers the acceptance criterion: it lives in
executions.spec.ts, verifies cost-tier information is rendered in the
execution detail view, and is wired into the same Playwright suite that runs
in CI.

What a follow-up would need
===========================
If the reviewer believes the existing test is insufficient, the follow-up
should specify which additional dimension is missing — e.g. asserting on a
distinct MinPower/MaxPower field that does not yet exist in the UI, or
asserting on the Cost cell from the execution-list row rather than the
detail view. As written, the AC ("MinPower/MaxPower OR cost tier
information is rendered in execution detail view; test passes in CI") is
satisfied by the cost-tier branch of the disjunction.
